title: browser: add streaming xlsx reader for blob
author: myfreeer
body:
## Summary When reading large xlsx from file, this could use less memory compared to reading the whole file into memory and extract it into memory. Since `Blob` is seekable, we can read it by part and adapt to streaming reader. workbook-reader-blob.js: the blob xlsx reader abstract-workbook-reader.js: extracted common part of `workbook-reader.js` and `workbook-reader-blob.js` blob-zip-stream.js: zip blob to stream reader modified from (MIT Licensed in package.json) ## Test plan A simple test is included in `spec/browser/exceljs.spec.js`. A profiling is done with `spec/integration/data/huge.xlsx` as input file with results below: ### Workbook.xlsx.load [Profile-load-20211120T104152.zip](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/files/7573798/Profile-load-20211120T104152.zip) ![load](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/17702502/142711964-ebc402d3-1f72-4cc2-b8d2-688f67bbc66d.PNG) ```html demo

``` ### WorkbookReader [Profile-stream-20211120T104141.zip](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/files/7573799/Profile-stream-20211120T104141.zip) ![stream](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/17702502/142711968-f41a4ff5-01f0-40c9-bdac-2c44b8fd2428.PNG) ```html demo

```

@coder-xiaotian This project does not seems to be actively maintained.

comments.author: coder-xiaotian
comments.body:
> > @myfreeer What is the status of this PR?
> 
> @coder-xiaotian This project does not seems to be actively maintained.

Have you found any other alternative solutions?

comments.author: Siemienik
myfreeer
comments.body:
> > > @myfreeer What is the status of this PR?
> > 
> > 
> > @coder-xiaotian This project does not seems to be actively maintained.
> 
> Have you found any other alternative solutions?

* [sheetjs](https://github.com/SheetJS/sheetjs) is an alternative excel library in pure js, with better full-buffer reading/writing performance, but lack of streaming support and cell styling.
* With modern browsers, it might be possible to compile native excel libraries to wasm, like [libxlsxwriter](https://github.com/jmcnamara/libxlsxwriter), [OpenXLSX](https://github.com/troldal/OpenXLSX), [xlnt](https://github.com/tfussell/xlnt), [unioffice](https://github.com/unidoc/unioffice), and more.

I know sheetjs, but it is not support stream api.  Anyway, thany you!

comments.author: myfreeer
comments.body:
> @coder-xiaotian We've started to organize ourselves for volunteer maintenance for this project. If you would like to join the conversation on Discord: https://discord.gg/GyWXEwv7 We're going to organize a live session to start very modestly working with ExcelJS, fork the project, set up the IDE, and tackle the first issue after not having done anything with this project for a long time. @naorpeled proposed to sit on this PR at the next meeting.
> 
> @myfreeer would you like to join us?

@Siemienik
Thank you for reaching out and inviting me, glad to hear that exceljs is going back to life. However, I am afraid that I will have to politely decline at this time. My schedule is currently quite busy, and I do not think that I have enough time for this. I'm coming back to this pr once I have enough time.

comments.author: Siemienik
comments.body:
@myfreeer 
> Thank you for reaching out and inviting me, glad to hear that exceljs is going back to life. However, I am afraid that I will have to politely decline at this time. My schedule is currently quite busy, and I do not think that I have enough time for this. I'm coming back to this pr once I have enough time.

Thank you for this PR, it brings a very nice and desired functionality. Don't worry about anything, you've done a lot and your code looks great and works great as well. If you don't mind, we will supplement this PR with documentation and merge it soon.

Regarding merge fests - I plan to do them regularly as long as there is enough energy and PRs.
More info on our Discord: https://discord.gg/BqTQGHDKw8

comments.author: myfreeer
comments.body:
> It looks impressive. Thank you for your collaboration. It would be nice to update Readme to add some info on how to use `.stream` API.
> 
> I approve your PR, and will merge soon after the docs are completed and the tests pass.

The stream API could be used just like the [stream api for node](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs#streaming-xlsx-readercontents), except that type of input is [`Blob`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Blob), an example could be found in [test added here](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/pull/1457/files#diff-6022806339144cd69e3ca70596b159157100d4faf26ee0a1c8c91d4cd36c9dc0R56)
